root_text.an7.52:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā campāyaṁ viharati gaggarāya pokkharaṇiyā tīre.
root_text.an7.52:1.2: Atha kho sambahulā campeyyakā upāsakā yena āyasmā sāriputto tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho campeyyakā upāsakā āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.an7.52:1.4: Sādhu mayaṁ, bhante, labheyyāma bhagavato sammukhā dhammiṁ kathaṁ savanāyā”ti.
root_text.an7.52:1.6: appeva nāma labheyyātha bhagavato sammukhā dhammiṁ kathaṁ savanāyā”ti.
root_text.an7.52:1.7: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho campeyyakā upāsakā āyasmato sāriputtassa paṭissutvā uṭṭhāyāsanā āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkamiṁsu.
root_text.an7.52:2.1: Atha kho campeyyakā upāsakā tadahuposathe yenāyasmā sāriputto tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhaṁsu.
root_text.an7.52:2.2: Atha kho āyasmā sāriputto tehi campeyyakehi upāsakehi saddhiṁ yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā sāriputto bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an7.52:3.1: “Siyā nu kho, bhante, idhekaccassa tādisaṁyeva dānaṁ dinnaṁ na mahapphalaṁ hoti na mahānisaṁsaṁ;
root_text.an7.52:3.2: siyā pana, bhante, idhekaccassa tādisaṁyeva dānaṁ dinnaṁ mahapphalaṁ hoti mahānisaṁsan”ti?
root_text.an7.52:3.3: “Siyā, sāriputta, idhekaccassa tādisaṁyeva dānaṁ dinnaṁ na mahapphalaṁ hoti na mahānisaṁsaṁ;
root_text.an7.52:3.4: siyā pana, sāriputta, idhekaccassa tādisaṁyeva dānaṁ dinnaṁ mahapphalaṁ hoti mahānisaṁsan”ti.
root_text.an7.52:3.5: “Ko nu kho, bhante, hetu ko paccayo yena midhekaccassa tādisaṁyeva dānaṁ dinnaṁ na mahapphalaṁ hoti na mahānisaṁsaṁ;
root_text.an7.52:3.6: ko nu kho, bhante, hetu ko paccayo yena midhekaccassa tādisaṁyeva dānaṁ dinnaṁ mahapphalaṁ hoti mahānisaṁsan”ti?
root_text.an7.52:4.1: “Idha, sāriputta, ekacco sāpekho dānaṁ deti, patibaddhacitto dānaṁ deti, sannidhipekho dānaṁ deti, ‘imaṁ pecca paribhuñjissāmī’ti dānaṁ deti.
root_text.an7.52:4.2: So taṁ dānaṁ deti samaṇassa vā brāhmaṇassa vā annaṁ pānaṁ vatthaṁ yānaṁ mālāgandhavilepanaṁ seyyāvasathapadīpeyyaṁ.
root_text.an7.52:4.3: Taṁ kiṁ maññasi, sāriputta, dadeyya idhekacco evarūpaṁ dānan”ti?
root_text.an7.52:5.1: “Tatra, sāriputta, yvāyaṁ sāpekho dānaṁ deti, patibaddhacitto dānaṁ deti, sannidhipekho dānaṁ deti, ‘imaṁ pecca paribhuñjissāmī’ti dānaṁ deti.
root_text.an7.52:5.2: So taṁ dānaṁ datvā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā cātumahārājikānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an7.52:5.3: So taṁ kammaṁ khepetvā taṁ iddhiṁ taṁ yasaṁ taṁ ādhipaccaṁ āgāmī hoti āgantā itthattaṁ.
root_text.an7.52:6.1: Idha pana, sāriputta, ekacco na heva kho sāpekho dānaṁ deti, na patibaddhacitto dānaṁ deti, na sannidhipekho dānaṁ deti, na ‘imaṁ pecca paribhuñjissāmī’ti dānaṁ deti;
root_text.an7.52:6.2: api ca kho ‘sāhu dānan’ti dānaṁ deti …pe….
root_text.an7.52:7.2: api ca kho ‘dinnapubbaṁ katapubbaṁ pitupitāmahehi na arahāmi porāṇaṁ kulavaṁsaṁ hāpetun’ti dānaṁ deti …pe….
root_text.an7.52:8.1: Napi ‘dinnapubbaṁ katapubbaṁ pitupitāmahehi na arahāmi porāṇaṁ kulavaṁsaṁ hāpetun’ti dānaṁ deti;
root_text.an7.52:8.2: api ca kho ‘ahaṁ pacāmi, ime na pacanti, nārahāmi pacanto apacantānaṁ dānaṁ adātun’ti dānaṁ deti …pe….
root_text.an7.52:9.1: Napi ‘ahaṁ pacāmi, ime na pacanti, nārahāmi pacanto apacantānaṁ dānaṁ adātun’ti dānaṁ deti;
root_text.an7.52:9.2: api ca kho ‘yathā tesaṁ pubbakānaṁ isīnaṁ tāni mahāyaññāni ahesuṁ, seyyathidaṁ—
root_text.an7.52:9.3: aṭṭhakassa vāmakassa vāmadevassa vessāmittassa yamadaggino aṅgīrasassa bhāradvājassa vāseṭṭhassa kassapassa bhaguno, evaṁ me ayaṁ dānasaṁvibhāgo bhavissatī’ti dānaṁ deti …pe….
root_text.an7.52:10.1: Napi ‘yathā tesaṁ pubbakānaṁ isīnaṁ tāni mahāyaññāni ahesuṁ, seyyathidaṁ—
root_text.an7.52:10.2: aṭṭhakassa vāmakassa vāmadevassa vessāmittassa yamadaggino aṅgīrasassa bhāradvājassa vāseṭṭhassa kassapassa bhaguno, evaṁ me ayaṁ dānasaṁvibhāgo bhavissatī’ti dānaṁ deti;
root_text.an7.52:10.3: api ca kho ‘imaṁ me dānaṁ dadato cittaṁ pasīdati, attamanatā somanassaṁ upajāyatī’ti dānaṁ deti …pe….
root_text.an7.52:11.1: Napi ‘imaṁ me dānaṁ dadato cittaṁ pasīdati, attamanatā somanassaṁ upajāyatī’ti dānaṁ deti;
root_text.an7.52:11.2: api ca kho cittālaṅkāracittaparikkhāraṁ dānaṁ deti.
root_text.an7.52:11.3: So taṁ dānaṁ deti samaṇassa vā brāhmaṇassa vā annaṁ pānaṁ vatthaṁ yānaṁ mālāgandhavilepanaṁ seyyāvasathapadīpeyyaṁ.
root_text.an7.52:11.4: Taṁ kiṁ maññasi, sāriputta, dadeyya idhekacco evarūpaṁ dānan”ti?
root_text.an7.52:12.1: “Tatra, sāriputta, yvāyaṁ na heva sāpekho dānaṁ deti;
root_text.an7.52:12.6: napi ‘dinnapubbaṁ katapubbaṁ pitupitāmahehi na arahāmi porāṇaṁ kulavaṁsaṁ hāpetun’ti dānaṁ deti;
root_text.an7.52:12.7: napi ‘ahaṁ pacāmi, ime na pacanti, nārahāmi pacanto apacantānaṁ dānaṁ adātun’ti dānaṁ deti;
root_text.an7.52:12.8: napi ‘yathā tesaṁ pubbakānaṁ isīnaṁ tāni mahāyaññāni ahesuṁ, seyyathidaṁ—
root_text.an7.52:12.9: aṭṭhakassa vāmakassa vāmadevassa vessāmittassa yamadaggino aṅgīrasassa bhāradvājassa vāseṭṭhassa kassapassa bhaguno, evaṁ me ayaṁ dānasaṁvibhāgo bhavissatī’ti dānaṁ deti;
root_text.an7.52:12.10: napi ‘imaṁ me dānaṁ dadato cittaṁ pasīdati, attamanatā somanassaṁ upajāyatī’ti dānaṁ deti;
root_text.an7.52:12.11: api ca kho cittālaṅkāracittaparikkhāraṁ dānaṁ deti.
root_text.an7.52:12.12: So taṁ dānaṁ datvā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā brahmakāyikānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an7.52:12.13: So taṁ kammaṁ khepetvā taṁ iddhiṁ taṁ yasaṁ taṁ ādhipaccaṁ anāgāmī hoti anāgantā itthattaṁ.
root_text.an7.52:13.1: Ayaṁ kho, sāriputta, hetu ayaṁ paccayo yena midhekaccassa tādisaṁyeva dānaṁ dinnaṁ na mahapphalaṁ hoti na mahānisaṁsaṁ.
root_text.an7.52:13.2: Ayaṁ pana, sāriputta, hetu ayaṁ paccayo yena midhekaccassa tādisaṁyeva dānaṁ dinnaṁ mahapphalaṁ hoti mahānisaṁsan”ti.

translation_text.an7.52:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Campā on the banks of the Gaggarā Lotus Pond.
translation_text.an7.52:1.2: Then several lay followers of Campā went to Venerable Sāriputta, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him,
translation_text.an7.52:1.3: “Sir, it’s been a long time since we’ve heard a Dhamma talk from the Buddha.
translation_text.an7.52:1.4: It would be good if we got to hear a Dhamma talk from the Buddha.”
translation_text.an7.52:1.5: “Well then, reverends, come on the next sabbath day.
translation_text.an7.52:1.6: Hopefully you’ll get to hear a Dhamma talk from the Buddha.”
translation_text.an7.52:1.7: “Yes, sir” they replied. Then they rose from their seats, bowed to Sāriputta, and respectfully circled him before leaving.
translation_text.an7.52:2.1: Then on the next sabbath the lay followers of Campā went to Venerable Sāriputta, bowed, and stood to one side.
translation_text.an7.52:2.2: Then they went together with Sāriputta to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. Sāriputta said to the Buddha:
translation_text.an7.52:3.1: “Sir, could it be that someone gives a gift and it is not very fruitful or beneficial,
translation_text.an7.52:3.2: while someone else gives exactly the same gift and it is very fruitful and beneficial?”
translation_text.an7.52:3.5: “Sir, what is the cause, what is the reason for this?”
translation_text.an7.52:4.1: “Sāriputta, take the case of a someone who gives a gift as an investment, their mind tied to it, expecting to keep it, thinking ‘I’ll enjoy this in my next life’.
translation_text.an7.52:4.2: They give to ascetics or brahmins such things as food, drink, clothing, vehicles; garlands, fragrance, and makeup; and bed, house, and lighting.
translation_text.an7.52:4.3: What do you think, Sāriputta, don’t some people give gifts in this way?”
translation_text.an7.52:5.1: “Sāriputta, someone who gives a gift as an investment,
translation_text.an7.52:5.2: when their body breaks up, after death, is reborn in the company of the gods of the four great kings.
translation_text.an7.52:5.3: When that deed, success, glory, and sovereignty is spent they return to this place.
translation_text.an7.52:6.1: Next, take the case of a someone who gives a gift not as an investment, their mind not tied to it, not expecting to keep it, and not thinking, ‘I’ll enjoy this in my next life’.
translation_text.an7.52:6.2: But they give a gift thinking, ‘It’s good to give’ …
translation_text.an7.52:7.2: They give a gift thinking, ‘Giving was practiced by my father and my father’s father. It would not be right for me to abandon this family tradition.’ …
translation_text.an7.52:8.2: They give a gift thinking, ‘I cook, they don’t. It wouldn’t be right for me to not give to them.’ …
translation_text.an7.52:9.2: They give a gift thinking, ‘The ancient brahmin seers were Aṭṭhaka, Vāmaka, Vāmadeva, Vessāmitta, Yamadaggi, Aṅgīrasa, Bhāradvāja, Vāseṭṭha, Kassapa, and Bhagu.
translation_text.an7.52:9.3: Just as they performed great sacrifices, I will share a gift.’ …
translation_text.an7.52:10.3: They give a gift thinking, ‘When giving this gift my mind becomes clear, and I become happy and joyful.’ …
translation_text.an7.52:11.1: They don’t give a gift thinking, ‘When giving this gift my mind becomes clear, and I become happy and joyful.’
translation_text.an7.52:11.2: But they give a gift thinking, ‘This is an adornment and requisite for the mind.’
translation_text.an7.52:11.3: They give to ascetics or brahmins such things as food, drink, clothing, vehicles; garlands, fragrance, and makeup; and bed, house, and lighting.
translation_text.an7.52:11.4: What do you think, Sāriputta, don’t some people give gifts in this way?”
translation_text.an7.52:12.1: “Sāriputta, someone who gives gifts, not for any other reason,
translation_text.an7.52:12.11: but thinking, ‘This is an adornment and requisite for the mind’,
translation_text.an7.52:12.12: when their body breaks up, after death, is reborn among the gods of the Divinity’s host.
translation_text.an7.52:12.13: When that deed, success, glory, and sovereignty is spent they are a non-returner; they do not return to this place.
translation_text.an7.52:13.1: This is the cause, this is the reason why someone gives a gift and it is not very fruitful or beneficial,
translation_text.an7.52:13.2: while someone else gives exactly the same gift and it is very fruitful and beneficial.”

variant_text.an7.52:1.4: bhagavato sammukhā dhammiṁ kathaṁ → bhagavato santikā dhammiṁ kathaṁ (bj); bhagavato dhammiṁ kathaṁ (sya-all); bhagavato sammukhā dhammikathaṁ (pts1ed)
variant_text.an7.52:4.1: sāpekho → sāpekkho (sya-all) | patibaddhacitto → paṭibaddhacitto (bj, sya1ed, sya2ed, pts1ed); patibandhacitto (mr)
variant_text.an7.52:12.1: na heva → naheva kho (bj, sya-all, pts1ed)
